title: Sliding Window Maximum
description:
Given an array nums and an integer k, find the maximum value in each sliding window of size k. Return all maximums.

Input format:
Line 1: N and K
Line 2: N space-separated integers

solutions.python:
from collections import deque
line1 = input().split()
n, k = int(line1[0]), int(line1[1])
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
dq = deque()
result = []
for i in range(n):
    while dq and dq[0] < i-k+1:
        dq.popleft()
    while dq and nums[dq[-1]] < nums[i]:
        dq.pop()
    dq.append(i)
    if i >= k-1:
        result.append(nums[dq[0]])
print(*result)

def lower(arr,t):
    l,r,res=0,len(arr)-1,-1
    while l<=r:
        m=(l+r)//2
        if arr[m]==t: res=m; r=m-1
        elif arr[m]<t: l=m+1
        else: r=m-1
    return res
def upper(arr,t):
    l,r,res=0,len(arr)-1,-1
    while l<=r:
        m=(l+r)//2
        if arr[m]==t: res=m; l=m+1
        elif arr[m]<t: l=m+1
        else: r=m-1
    return res
print(lower(a,target),upper(a,target))

title: Trapping Rain Water
description:
Given N non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1,
compute how much water it can trap after raining.

Input:

Line 2: N integers (heights)
hints: Two-pointer approach: left and right pointers move inward.
Water at position i = min(maxLeft, maxRight) - height[i]
Move the pointer with smaller max towards center.

solutions.python:
n = int(input())
h = list(map(int, input().split()))
l, r = 0, n-1
maxL = maxR = water = 0
while l < r:
    if h[l] < h[r]:
        if h[l] >= maxL: maxL = h[l]
        else: water += maxL - h[l]
        l += 1
    else:
        if h[r] >= maxR: maxR = h[r]
        else: water += maxR - h[r]
        r -= 1
print(water)

    print(s)

        else: res.append(s[i-1]+str(cnt)); cnt=1

def merge_sort(arr):
    if len(arr) <= 1: return arr
    mid = len(arr) // 2
    L = merge_sort(arr[:mid])
    R = merge_sort(arr[mid:])
    res, i, j = [], 0, 0
    while i < len(L) and j < len(R):
        if L[i] <= R[j]: res.append(L[i]); i+=1
        else: res.append(R[j]); j+=1
    return res + L[i:] + R[j:]

title: Quick Sort
description:
Sort an array of N integers using Quick Sort. Print sorted array.

Key Interview Facts:
• NOT stable (elements with equal keys may change order)
• Average Time: O(N log N) — preferred in practice
• Worst Time: O(N²) — when pivot is always min/max (rare with good pivot strategy)
• Space: O(log N) — recursive call stack

Input:
Line 1: N
Line 2: N integers
hints: Choose a pivot (last element is simplest).
Partition: all elements < pivot go left, > pivot go right.
Recursively sort left and right partitions.

solutions.python:
def quick_sort(arr, lo, hi):
    if lo >= hi: return
    pivot = arr[hi]
    i = lo - 1
    for j in range(lo, hi):
        if arr[j] <= pivot:
            i += 1
            arr[i], arr[j] = arr[j], arr[i]
    arr[i+1], arr[hi] = arr[hi], arr[i+1]
    p = i + 1
    quick_sort(arr, lo, p-1)
    quick_sort(arr, p+1, hi)
n = int(input())

quick_sort(arr, 0, n-1)
print(*arr)
